# TESS Program G08101

Title: Assessing The Difference Between Planetary Systems Around A-Type And Gkm-Type Stars: The 10-40 Day Period Regime

PI: Melis, Carl - University Of California (San Diego)

Type: LARGE

Summary: With TESS data products we seek to comprehensively establish whether planetary systems observed around GKM-type stars are allowable or ruled out for A-type stars. We propose to examine all A-type stars brighter than 12th magnitude that will enjoy 80-day monitoring for the presence of any transiting planets. This will require the best lightcurve sensitivity possible for A-type stars to try and mitigate their larger size relative to GKM-type stars and we will employ a proven methodology for teasing exoplanet transits out of pulsating star lightcurves when necessary to ensure sufficient intercomparison with GKM-type stars. The proposed investigation will deliver robust exoplanet demographics in the 10-40 day period range for otherwise ignored A-type stars.
